Title: Genome-wide association study reveals that the cupin domain protein OsCDP3.10 regulates seed vigour in rice

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: amino acids (especially Met, Glu, His, Tyr); 52 kDa globulin
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Amino acids support embryonic and seedling development and contribute to H2O2 accumulation that promotes seed germination and seedling establishment.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing grains and germinating seeds; increases during grain filling to peak around 28 DAF then declines; decreases during germination; strong GUS in developing shoot and radicle.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing grains and germinating seeds; increases during grain filling to peak around 28 DAF then declines; decreases during germination; strong GUS in developing shoot and radicle.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Disruption reduces seed vigour, including germination potential, seedling percentage, and seedling establishment, under normal and salt stress; causes loss of 52 kDa globulin accumulation and lowers amino acid contents in mature grains and germinating seeds.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS candidate-gene identification; haplotype analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; qRT-PCR; promoter-GUS; SDS-PAGE for 52 kDa globulin; amino acid profiling; H2O2 assay; amino-acid rescue treatments; seed-priming assays
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: OsCDP3.10 promoter (~1.9–1 kb upstream, native promoter for GUS); constitutive promoter for overexpression not reported
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS with ~700,000 SNPs (399,537 analyzed after filtering); expression database mining using Rice eFP Browser and Genevestigator
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal germination conditions; salt stress (150 mM NaCl); exogenous amino acid treatments (Met, Glu, His, Tyr and others); seed priming with amino acids
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: OsCDP3.10 overexpression did not significantly increase seed vigour, suggesting cofactor dependence; mutants showed reduced soluble sugar content, but no major changes in grain size, weight, starch, or total protein.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS identified qSP3, and LOC_Os03g57960/OsCDP3.10 was validated as the causal candidate gene for seedling percentage and seed vigour. 2. CRISPR disruption of OsCDP3.10 reduced early germination vigour and seedling establishment under both normal and salt stress conditions. 3. Mutants lacked 52 kDa globulin accumulation and had reduced levels of multiple amino acids in mature grains and germinating seeds. 4. Met, Glu, His, and Tyr treatments partially rescued mutant seed vigour and increased H2O2 in germinating seeds. 5. Natural allelic variation of OsCDP3.10 was associated with differences in seedling percentage and globulin accumulation among rice accessions.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper describes OsCDP3.10 as a cupin domain protein functioning in synthesis of 52 kDa globulin, but it is not presented as a classical catalytic pathway enzyme or transcriptional regulator. The elite haplotype Hap 1 is associated with higher seed vigour, whereas Hap 2 is associated with lower seed vigour mainly in japonica accessions. Coding SNP 4 is linked to seedling percentage variation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for marker-assisted selection of elite OsCDP3.10 haplotypes to improve seed vigour in direct-seeded rice; amino-acid seed priming (especially Met, Glu, His, Tyr) can complement low-vigour genotypes and improve performance under salt stress; overexpression alone may be insufficient without additional cofactors.
